ety: S. तडाक
def: s.m. Pond, pool, tank, reservoir of water ( = talāʼo; cf. tāl).
more: n. m. S. तडाग , Pr. talāam . A tank; pond; pool. Tāl to Bhopāl tāl, aur sab talaiyāṅ haiṅ . Kabit. The only tank's Bhopal's, the rest are only ponds.
s. m. A pond, a reservoir of water, a tank. (Sans. तडाग ).

ety: inf. n. of طلب 'to seek, desire,' &c.
def: s.f. Search, quest; wish, desire; inquiry, request, demand, application, solicitation; sending for, summons; an object of quest, or of desire; — pay, wages, salary (syn. tanḵẖẉāh); — act. part. & s.m. (fr. P. t̤alab-īdan), Seeking, requiring, desiring; — seeker, desirer (used as last member of comp., e.g. ārām-t̤alab, q.v.s.v. ārām)
more: n. f. 1. ( In compos .) Seeking; seeker; requiring. 2. Wish, etc. ( chāh ). Talab lagī huī hai . 3. Solicitation ( ardās , 1). 4. Pay ( tankhwāh ). Golā bārūt kahīṅ jāë, talab se kām . Prov. Be balls and gunpowder discharged wheree'er they may, my business only is to draw my pay. talab bāṅṭnā , v. a. To distribute pay. talab bujhānā , v. a. To satisfy one's desire or appetite. Huqqā pī-kar apnī talab bujhā lo! talab-dār , n. m. 1. One addicted to drinking, smoking, etc. 2. One who has a fixed salary or wages. talab denā , v. a. To pay one his wages or salary. talab k. , v. a. 1. To call for ( bulānā , 1, 2). 2. To demand; claim. 3. To exact. talab-gār , adj. Seeking; desirous of. talab-gār , n. m. A seeker; claimant. talab-nāma , n. m. A summons ( safīnah ). ārām-talab , n. m. 1. One who is fond of ease and comfort. 2. A lazy fellow; an idler. mukarrar talab k. , v. a. 1. To call again; …

1. s. f. Pay, wages, salary. 2. Desire, wish, inquiry, search, demand, request, application, sending for, summons. 3. part. act. (of طلبيدن) in compos. Seeking, seeker, requiring. طلب کرنا t̤alab karnā, To demand, seek, require, ask for, call, summon.
subs: adj. & s.m. Asking, inquiring; — desirous; — a seeker or searcher; one who seeks the gratification of desire; one who is addicted to drinking, or smoking, &c.
or who has a fixed salary or wages
v.t. To seek; to summon, cite, call for; to require, demand, claim; to exact
adj. & s.m. Seeking, desirous (of, -kā); — seeker, searcher; desirer; claimant
(-kā), To be a seeker (of, or after), to seek, &c.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian طَلَب (talab), from Arabic طَلَب (ṭalab).

def: seeker; demander
wety: From طَلَب (talab, “seek, demand”) + ـگار (-gār).

def: oblique plural of طَلَب گَار (talab gār)
vocative plural of طَلَب گَار (talab gār)

ety: for A. طلبة, pl. of t̤ālib
def: s.m. Students (see t̤ulabā).
more: (or t̤ulaba for طلبا t̤ulabā ) s. plur. (of طالب) Inquisitive, the studious, students.

ety: t̤alab, q.v.+Zend āna or ana = S. अन
def: s.m. Fee paid to a peon for serving a process; the fee payable to a witness; daily pay to constables, &c. (paid by those they guard).
more: n. m. 1. Fees to peons for serving process. 2. The fee payable to a witness. 3. Daily pay to constables, etc. talbānah dākhil k. , v. a. To pay witness's fees.
s. m. Daily pay to constables &c. paid by those they guard, &c.

def: lockdown, shutdown, lockout
wety: From تالا (tālā, “lock”) + بندی (bandī, “closing, shutting”).

def: 1. adj. Desirous. 2. part. act. Asking, inquiring. 3. s. m. One who receives pay.
adj. Desirous; (substant.) A seeker.
s. f. Desiring, wishing, inquiring, seeking.
oblique plural of طَلَبْگار (talabgār)
vocative plural of طَلَبْگار (talabgār)
adj. Requisite, desirable; — s.f. Request, demand; summons ( = t̤alab)
more: n. f. Summons ( bulāvā ). talbī h. , v. n. To be called or summoned. ziyādah-talabī , n. f. Excessive demand; over-assessment.
subs: v.n. To be cited, be summoned

ety: perf. part. of t̤alabīdan, fr. t̤alab
def: part. adj. Sought, desired, &c.
more: (part. pass. of طلبيدن) adj. Sought, called.

def: s.m. Perishing; ruin, destruction, loss; profusion, prodigality, waste, consumption, expense
more: n. m. تلف he perished. Waste; loss; destruction.

s. m. 1. Ruin, destruction, loss. 2. Prodigality, consumption, expense. تلف المال خلف العمر talafu-l-māl ḵẖalafu-l-uﻌmr, The loss of riches is the prolongation of life. تلف کرنا talaf karnā, To ruin, to waste, consume, destroy. تلف هونا talaf honā, To be unfortunate, to meet with a loss, to be destroyed.
subs: The loss of riches is the prolongation of life
v.t. To ruin, destroy; to waste, consume
v.n. To perish, to be destroyed or ruined; to be wasted; to meet with a loss, be unfortunate.

ety: inf. n. v of لفظ 'to throw out (of the mouth)'
def: s.m. Pronunciation, articulation, utterance, expression.
more: n. m. لفظ throwing out of the mouth. Articulation; pronunciation; accent.
(inf. v. of لفظ) s. m. Utterance, expression, pronunciation.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian تَلَفُّظ (talaffuz), from Arabic تَلَفُّظ (talaffuẓ).

ety: inf. n. vi of لفو
def: s.f. Making amends, reparation, compensation, recompense.
more: (inf. vi. of لفو) s. f. Compensation, recompense, reparation, making amends.

The palmyra-tree or fan-palm ( = tāṛ, q.v.); — clapping the hands together; slapping the hands on the arms in defiance (in wrestling = tāṛī, q.v.); beating time (in music); musical time or measure; chime; a musical instrument of bell-metal or brass, a sort of cymbal (played with a stick); a pair of cymbals; — the glass or pebble of an eye-glass

tāl-vr̤int, s.m. A palm-leaf used as a fan; a fan.

ety: Prk. तलाइआ = S. तडाक+इका
def: s.f. A small tank or pond ( = talaiyā).

ety: talā˚, rt. of talānā+āʼī = Prk. एअव्वअइआ = S. (इ)तव्य+ता+इका(तिका)

ety: S. तल+स्थ+इका
def: s.f. The bottom or lower part (of anything); the bed (of a river); the floor (of a room); outskirts, suburbs, environs.
n. f. 1. The foot of a hill. 2. The floor of a room. 3. The bed of a river. 4. Suburbs; environs.

ety: Prk. ता = S. तावत+lay; but see tak
def: postpn. To, up to, &c. = tak, q.v. s.f. Look, regard, glance, gaze; fixed look, stare; peep, watch, look-out, expectation; view, aim, the act of aiming (at); pointing (of a dog, &c.)
more: adv. Till: see تک tak, sign. 1.
subs: s.f. Mutual gazing or staring, exchange of glances; bo-peep
s.f. Precise moment, nick of time
(-par), To fix the gaze (on), look fixedly (at); to take aim (at); to point (as a dog, &c.); to look longingly (at), to covet
(-kī), To come in expectation (of), to come with an eye (to)
tāk-meṅ rahnā (-kī), To be on the look out or watch (for), to lie in wait (for).

ety: inf. n. of طلق 'to be set free, be separated from,' &c.
def: s.f. Divorcing (a woman); divorce; repudiation
more: (rend), n. f. طلق loosed, separated. 1. A divorce; repudiation. Rizāle kī jorū ko sadā talāq . Prov. Low men's wives are perpetually put away. 2. Oath ( qasam ). Tere jantī par talāq hai! talāq ba-nān o nafqa. A mensā et toro ; from bed and board. talāq denā , v. a. To divorce; repudiate; put away (a wife). talāq-nāma . A deed of divorce. talāq'ī, talāqan . A divorce; one divorced.
subs: (-ko), To divorce, to put away (a wife); to repudiate
s.m. A writing or a bill of divorcement, a deed of divorce.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian طلاق (talāq), from Arabic طَلَاق (ṭalāq).

ety: rel. n. fr. t̤alāk; — aff. -an = S. इनी
def: A person divorced, divorcee.

ety: for A. طلاقة, inf. n. of طلق 'to become eloquent,' &c.
def: s.f. Eloquence; freedom or glibness (of tongue).
more: s. f. Eloquence or freedom (of tongue).

ety: tāla+ākhyā
def: s.f. See s.v. tāl. s.m. Pond, pool, tank, lake ( = tālāb).

ety: inf. n. vi of لقي 'to meet'
def: s.f. Meeting each other; meeting, reunion, interview.
more: (inf. vi. of لقي or لفو) s. f. Meeting, interview, reunion.

ety: rel. n. fr. t̤alāk; — aff. -an = S. इनी
def: A person divorced, divorcee.
s.f. = talmalī, q.v. s.f. Restlessness; fidgetiness; impatience
subs: (-ko), To suffer from restlessness, to be restless or uneasy, &c.

def: (contrac. of talāmīẕ) = P تلامذه talāmiẕa (for A. تلامذة) = A تلاميذ talāmīẕ s.m. plur. (of tilmīẕ), Scholars, pupils, disciples).
= H تلامذ talāmiẕ (contrac. of talāmīẕ) = P تلامذه talāmiẕa (for A. تلامذة) s.m. plur. (of tilmīẕ), Scholars, pupils, disciples).
(for A. تلامذة) = H تلامذ talāmiẕ (contrac. of talāmīẕ) = A تلاميذ talāmīẕ s.m. plur. (of tilmīẕ), Scholars, pupils, disciples).
(dakh.) Against the grain ( Harris ).

ety: S. तडनं, rt. तड्; cf. also the S. तलित 'fried'
def: s.m. Frying; food or meat fried (in butter or oil).

ety: caus. of talnā; tal˚+ā = āw = Prk. आव or आवे S. = आपि caus. increment
def: v.t. To cause to fry; to have (a thing) fried.
more: v. a. See تَالنا 1. To cause to disappear or give way; to remove. Bātoṅ meṅ ṭalā dīyā . He put him off with fine words. 2. To pass in idleness; beguile one's time.

ety: caus. of ṭalnā
def: v.t. To cause to move or give way or flinch, to move out of the way, to remove; to put out of the way, cause to disappear, to conceal; to put off, evade; to pass in idleness, to beguile (time; — cf. ṭālnā)
more: (caus. of ٿلنا) v. a. To cause to disappear, to cause to give away.

ety: Prk. तलाउ or तलाओ = S. तडाकः
def: s.m. Tank, pond, &c. ( = tālāb).
more: (corrupt. of تالاب) s. m. A pond, reservoir of water.

def: s.f. Bit, piece, morsel, fragment, chip, paring.
more: s. f. A piece, a fragment.

def: v.n. = taṛaphnā or taṛapnā, q.v. v.n. = taṛapnā, q.v. v.n. To roll or toss about restlessly or uneasily; to flounder, flounce; to be agitated; to flutter, beat, palpitate, throb; to quiver, vibrate, writhe, wriggle; to jump, spring, bound; to be anxiously eager (for), be eagerly desirous, to long (for).
more: v. a. To flutter, palpitate, be agitated, to flounce.

def: v.n. = taṛapnā, q.v. v.n. To roll or toss about restlessly or uneasily; to flounder, flounce; to be agitated; to flutter, beat, palpitate, throb; to quiver, vibrate, writhe, wriggle; to jump, spring, bound; to be anxiously eager (for), be eagerly desirous, to long (for).
more: v. a. To flutter, palpitate, be agitated, to flounce.

def: (dakh.) See تلي tale.

ety: prob. contrac. from A. talāshī, q.v.
def: s.f. Search, quest; scrutiny; study, research; pursuit (syn. just-o-jū)
more: n. f. 1. Search; quest; scrutiny; investigation. 2. Pursuit; research; exploration. talāsh k . v. n. 1. To seek; search; look for; look about for. 2. To explore; reconnoitre; investigate. talāsh-i-maāsh , Search after a livelihood.
(see تلاشي) s. f. Search. تلاش کرنا talāsh karnā, To seek, to search for.
subs: (-kī), To search (for), to seek; to explore.

def: s.f. Searcihng the person, property, or house (of a person, for stolen property, or for smuggled goods); — s.m.f. Seeker, searcher
more: n. f. Searching one's person, property, or house; searching for dutiable or smuggled goods. talāshī le'nā , v. n. To institute a search; search the person or house; search for smuggled goods. khānah-talāshī , n. f. Searching a house for stolen property, or for smuggled goods.
(inf. vi. of لشي from لاشي Nothing) s. f. 1. Annihilation. 2. Dispersion.
subs: (-kī), To search the person or house, &c. (of); to institute a search.

ety: inf. n. vi of لشي; but said to be derived from لاشي lā-shay, 'nothing'
def: s.f. Annihilation; vanishing; disap earance; dispersion.
v. a. (dakh.) To rub.

ety: for A. طلعة, v.n. fr. طلع 'to appear,' &c.
def: s.f. Appearance, aspect, countenance, face.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian طلعت (tal'at), from Arabic طَلْعَة (ṭalʕa).

def: s. m. (dakh.) 1. East. 2. See نيچي nīche.
s.m. See s.v. tal. s.m. Pond, pool, tank, lake ( = tālāb).
more: n. S. तलातल The 4th of the seven lower regions.
s. m. The region under the earth.

ety: inf. n. v of لطف 'to be propitious or favourable'
def: s.m. Favouring, showing kindness; favour, kindness, obligingness; blandishment
more: (inf. v. of لطف) s. m. Kindness, obligingness, blandishment, favour. تلطف کرنا talat̤t̤uf karnā, To shew kindness or favour, to oblige.
subs: v.n. To show favour or kindness (to), to oblige.

ety: inf. n. vi of لطم 'to buffet'
def: s.m. Buffeting, dashing (particularly of waves).
more: (inf. vi. of لطم) s. m. Collision, dashing, buffeting (particularly, of waves).

def: s.m. = tālāb, q.v. s.m. Pond, pool, tank, reservoir of water ( = talāʼo; cf. tāl).
more: s. m. A pond, a reservoir of water, a tank. (Sans. तडाग ).

ety: corr. of P. tilāwa, which is a corr. of A. t̤alīʻa or of its pl. t̤alāʻe
def: s.m. Advanced guard; reconuoitering party, patrol, guard, picket, night-watch.
more: n. m. Cor. of T. طلايه An advanced guard; a patrol; a reconnoitring party.

ety: for A. طلاوة, v.n. fr. طلو; see t̤ilā
def: s.f. Grace, beauty, elegance, loveliness, attractiveness.
more: (from طلا) s. f. Grace, beauty, elegance.

ety: inf. n. v of لون; derived from laun, 'colour'
def: s.m. Changing colour (like a chameleon); versatility, variableness, changeableness, fickleness, capriciousness, caprice; restlessness, fidgetiness
more: n. m. A. لون color. 1. Changing colours (like a chameleon). 2. Changeableness; fickleness; capriciousness. talavvun-mizāj, mutalavvun , adj. Variable; fickle; unstable; capricious.

(inf. v. of لون) s. m. Changing colours (like a chameleon). 2. Fidgetting, restlessness, capriciousness, changeableness. تلون طبع يا مزاج talauwūn-t̤abﻌ or -mizāj, adj. Capricious, whimsical, fretful. تلون مزاجي talauwun-mizāj-ī, s. f. Capriciousness, caprice.
subs: talawwun-mizāj, adj. Versatile, changeable, unstable, capricious, fickle, whimsical

def: adj. Relating to the palate, palatal

ety: corr. of A. طلائع, pl. of طليعة, rt. طلع
def: s.m. Night-watch, rounds, picket, patrol; advanced guard, reconnoitering party.
more: (corrupt. of طليعه or طلايع) s. m. Night-watch, rounds, piquet, patrole.

ety: inf. n. v of لذّ 'to take pleasure'
def: s.m. Taking pleasure; pleasure, delight, enjoyment, charms, deliciousness.
more: (inf. v. of لذّ) s. m. Delight, pleasure, charms, deliciousness.

ety: inf. n. ii of لبس 'to cover,' &c.

Covering; obscuring, mystifying; concealing (facts or defects), deceiving, cheating; deception, fraud, cheat, imposture; fallacy; misrepresentation; failing to prove an allegation; false personation; knavery; a knave; confusion; mixture, adulteration, falsification, corruption, counterfeiting (coin).
more: n. f. لبس 1. False personation. 2. Counterfeiting coin.

(inf. ii. of لبس) s. f. 1. Cheat, fraud, fallacy, imposture. 2. Mixture, confusion. 3. Misrepresentation. 4. Concealing (the truth, the faults of goods on sale). 5. Failing in proving oneʼs allegations. 6. A knave. 7. Covering.

more: s. f. Dregs, refuse, scum, sediment, offals, leavings.

adj. Uneasy; restless; disquieted.
s.m. = H تلچهٿ तलछट tal-ćhaṭ, s.f. See s.v. tal.
more: n. f. 1. Light land above clay, which soon becomes soft and spongy in wet weather. 2. Menstruation.

The palmyra-tree or fan-palm ( = tāṛ, q.v.); — clapping the hands together; slapping the hands on the arms in defiance (in wrestling = tāṛī, q.v.); beating time (in music); musical time or measure; chime; a musical instrument of bell-metal or brass, a sort of cymbal (played with a stick); a pair of cymbals; — the glass or pebble of an eye-glass

Prov. The earth below and God above. (An oath). 2. Down; adown; downwards. 3. Under; next in order, degree, or rank. tale ūpar, ūpar tale , adv. 1. One upon the other ( ūpar tale ). 2. To fall under; to be crushed under. 2. To lie on one's back in coitu . 3. To sleep on the ground.

s.m. A medicinal application to the eyes

v.t. To chime; to slap the arms ( = tāl ṭhoṅknā)
v.n. To get, or to be, out of tune
s.m. The medicinal herb Barleria (or Asteracantha) longifolia; the seeds of Solanum indicum (see makhānā)
s.m. Harmony, agreement, accord, &c. ( = ittifāq, q.v.); — tāl-mel khānā, v.n. To be in harmony, to agree, &c. ( = ittifāq honā)
tāl-vr̤int, s.m. A palm-leaf used as a fan; a fan.

ety: act. part. of طلع 'to rise, to appear,' &c.
def: part. adj. Rising, appearing (as the sun), arising; — s.m. Star, destiny, fate, lot, fortune; prosperity; — the (false) dawn
more: n. m. طلع rose. Lot; star ( bhāg , 3). tālٴa-shinās , n. m. A fortune-teller; soothsayer. tāle-var, tāle-mand , adj. Fortunate ( dhanī , 1, 2).

part. act. (of طلع) 1. Rising (as, the sun), arising, appearing. 2. s. m. Fortune, prosperity. 3. Destiny (good or bad), horoscope. 4. The dawn. طالع آزماٴي t̤āliﻌ-āzmā-ī, s. f. Trying oneʼs fortune. طالع شناس t̤āliﻌ-shinās, Fortuneteller, astrologer. طالع مند t̤āliﻌ-mand, adj, 1. Fortunate. 2. Wealthy. طالع مندي t̤āliﻌ-mand-ī, s. f. 1. Fortune, prosperity. 2. Wealth. طالع ور t̤āliﻌ-war, adj. 1. Fortunate. 2. Wealthy. طالع وري t̤āliﻌ-war-ī, s. f. Good fortune.

adj. Fortunate; wealthy:-t̤ālěʻ-mandī, s.f. Fortune, prosperity; wealth

more: n. f. 1. (S. ताली ) A key kunjī ). 2. (S. ताल ) Clapping of the hands together. Ek hāth se tālī nahīṅ bajtī . Prov. It takes two hands to clap. (It takes two to make a quarrel). tālī bajā'nā, yā pīṭ'nā , v. a. 1. To clap one's hands (in approbation, rejoicing, or derision). Tāliyāṅ, bajā le, banno bā hogā . Nursery rhyme. Now clap your hands my baby dear, some one to wed you will come here. 2. To rejoice; to be joyful. par-tālī , n. f. A duplicate key.
n. f. S. घण्टाली 1. A small bell. 2. ( Brokers .) Eight annas.

s. f. 1. A key. 2. Clapping of the hands together. تالي ايک هاتهـ سي بجني tālī ek hāth se bajnī, is used to express an impossibility. تالي بجانا tālī bajānā, To clap the hands (generally, by way of censure and ridicule); to hoot. تالي مارنا tālī mārnā, v. a. To hoot, to damn.
wety: From تالا (tālā, “lock”) + ـی (-ī).

def: s.f. A sort of instrument for opening a door; a key; — the tree Corypha taliera; a species of the mountain-palm; the plant Flacourtia cataphracta.
wety: From تالا (tālā, “lock”) + ـی (-ī).

ety: S. तालिका
def: s.f. Clapping the hands; tālī-bajānā, tālī pīṭnā, tālī mārnā, v.n. To clap the hands (in order to attract attention, or in approbation, or in delight, or in derision); to beat time by clapping the hands; to rejoice; to applaud; to deride, ridicule, to hoot
subs: lit. 'A clapping to be produced with one hand'; an impossibility to be attempted: tālī ek hāth-se (or ek hāth-se tālī) nahīṅ bajtī, prov., It takes two hands to clap, 'it takes two to make a quarrel.'
wety: From تالا (tālā, “lock”) + ـی (-ī).

ety: Prk. तलिआ = S. तल+इका
def: s.f. Bottom; sole of a shoe, &c. ( = talā, q.v.); hoof of a camel; fine or impalpable powder
more: n. f. H. tal bottom. See تلا 1. 2. Na tel talī, na ūpar palī . Prov. Nor oil at the bottom nor even in the ladle. (Said to express extreme poverty).
s. f. 1. The bottom of any thing. 2. The sole of a shoe. 3. Beneath, under. 4. Impalpable powder. ¶ تلي tillī, s. f. The spleen or milt. تپ تلي tap-tillī, s. f. Disorder of the spleen.

adv. Below, down, beneath, under. تلي اوپر tale-ūpar, adj. 1. Upside down. 2. Confused. تلي تلي ديکهنا tale tale dekhnā, To see clandestinely. تلي کي زمين اوپرهوني tale kī zamīn ūpar honī, is used to express great disturbance and confusion.
subs: s.f. A thorough cleaning or sweeping out, a clean sweep.

ety: S. ताल+इका
def: s.f. A small bell ( = ṭāl); a half-rupee piece (in brokers' cant).
more: s. f. A sort of musical instrument. See تال tāl.

def: s.m. = P طليعت t̤alīʻat, s.f. (for A. طليعة, fr. طلع 'to rise,' &c.), Scout; party of scouts; a spy; vanguard; advanced post, picket; reconnoitering party; the forlorn hope (cf. t̤alāya).
more: s. m. The piquet or advanced post, the vanguard.

def: s.f. = P طليعه t̤alīʻa, s.m. (for A. طليعة, fr. طلع 'to rise,' &c.), Scout; party of scouts; a spy; vanguard; advanced post, picket; reconnoitering party; the forlorn hope (cf. t̤alāya).

ety: act. part. of طلب 'to seek,' &c.
def: part. adj. & s.m. Seeking, desiring, desirous, craving, demanding, asking, interrogating; — a seeker, an inquirer, a desirer, an asker, &c.
more: n. m. طلب sought. A seeker; an inquirer. tālib-i-dīdār , Poet. n. m. Suitor; lover. tālib-i-dunyā . Worldly-minded. tālib-i-zar , adj. Fond of money; mercenary; sordid. 1. Insāf ke khuāhāṅ haiṅ, nahīṅ tālib-i-zar ham, Tahsīn-i-sakhun-fahm hai, Momin, silah apnā . Momin. Justice I seek, of riches I 've no greed, The praise of scholars, Momin, be thy meed! 2. Tālib-i-zar be-zarūr, jag meṅ khuār, Haq se dūr . The greed for wealth excessive shown, by the world despised, from God far gone. tālib-i-uqbā . One who seeks after Heaven. tālib-i-ilm, tālib-ilm, tālib-ul-ilm , n. m. lit. searcher after knowledge. A student ( biddiyārthī ). tālib-ilmī , n. f. 1. Search after knowledge; study. 2. Pupilage. 3. Apprenticeship. tālib o matlūb , Lovers ( āshiq o māshūq , 1).

part. act. (of طلب) 1. Asking, demanding, interrogating. 2. s. m. An inquirer, a seeker, a lover, asker, candidate, exacter. طالب دنيا t̤ālib-i-dunyā, Worldly-minded. طالب علم t̤ālib-i-iﻌlm, Searching after knowledge, a student. طالب علمي t̤ālib-i-iﻌlm-ī, or طالب العلمي t̤ālibu-l-iﻌlm-ī, s. f. Search of knowledge, study. (Plur. Pers. طالبان Arab. طلبا).
subs: adj. & s.m. Worldly-minded; — one who seeks worldly goods or pleasures, &c.
s.m. One who longs for the sight (of), a suitor, a lover
or t̤ālib-ě-ʻilm, or t̤ālib-ʻilm, s.m. A seeker after knowledge, a student of science or knowledge; a student
or t̤ālib-ě-ʻilmī or t̤ālib-ʻilmī, s.f. Search after knowledge, study; — the state of being a student, pupilage

def: student

ety: inf. n. ii of الف 'to cleave to,' 'to like,' &c.
def: s.f. Uniting, connecting, bringing together; uniting in friendship, reconciling; conciliating, winning; — collecting, compiling; compilation, composition; — tālīf-ě-qulūb, s.f. Uniting hearts in friendship; conciliating or winning hearts, or the art of doing so
more: n. f. الف bringing together. Compilation. tālīf-i-qulūb , n. f. Winning the hearts of the people. Sarkār Angrezī meṅ tālīf-i-qulūb nahīṅ hai . The English have not the gift of winning hearts.
(inf. ii. of الفت) s. f. 1. Connecting, uniting in friendship, reconciling. 2. Composition, compilation (of a book, &c.). تاليف کرنا tālīf karnā, To compile, compose (a book, &c.).
subs: v.t. To unite in friendship, to reconcile; to conciliate, win; — to compile, compose (a book, &c.).

(inf. ii. of علت) s. f. 1. Causing one to make an excuse, occasioning. 2. Changing one of the weak letters for another (in grammar). Plur. تعليلات.

(inf. ii. of علم) s. f. 1. Teaching, instruction, tuition. 2. Copying fine, writing accurately. تعليم کرنا taﻌlīm karnā, To teach.

more: s. m. Impetus sive succussus in coitu. ٿلي نويسي ṭalle-navīs-ī, s. f. Unprofitable employment, wasting of time.

v. n. 1. To be tantalized. 2. To grieve, to be agitated.
v. n. To toss about (from sickness, &c.).

1. To totter. 2. To tantalize.
s.f. Restlessness; fidgetiness; impatience
more: n. f. Restlessness; impatience. talmalī lagnā , v. n. To be uneasy or restless.

def: v. n. (dakh.) To toss about (from sickness, &c.: Harris ).
s. m. Evasion, prevarication, putting off, shuffling, chicane.

ety: ṭāl˚ = S. तारय(ति), rt. तॄ
def: v.t. To pass over, go beyond, exceed (a fixed time); to put off, defer, postpone; to reject (a request); to elude by subterfuge, to evade, prevaricate; to avoid; to put or turn aside, to put or turn (one) out of the way, to deter, frighten away; to remove, avert, ward off, fend, obviate, prevent
more: (trans. of ٿلنا) v. a. 1. To evade, prevaricate, to avoid, put off, postpone, defer. 2. To drive out of the way, put or turn aside, ward off, fend, obviate, move, remove, prevent.
wety: Urdu verb set ٹَلْنا (ṭalnā) ٹالْنا (ṭālnā) Inherited from Sauraseni Prakrit 𑀝𑀸𑀮𑀤𑀺 (ṭāladi, “removes, frightens away”), from Sanskrit टालयति (ṭālayati, “cause to move”).

ety: ṭal˚ = S. तर(ति), rt. तॄ; cf. ṭālnā
def: v.n. To move, stir; to be displaced, be dislocated (as a bone, &c.); to give way, to shrink, flinch; to retire, make off, get out of the way, sheer off, decamp, disappear, vanish; to pass off, pass over, pass by or away (as a fixed time or season for anything, or a danger or evil); to be evaded or shirked; to fail of observance, to withdraw, draw back or out (of a promise or agreement, &c.)
more: v. n. 1. To give way, shrink from, flinch. 2. To disappear, pass away, retire.

more: v. a. To fry or be fried in butter or oil.

v. n. 1. To move; stir; take oneself off; disappear; vanish. 1. Kāl ṭale, par katāl na ṭale . Prov. Death may be put off, but the bottle never. (The drunkard makes death wait till he has obeyed the summons from the ale-house). 2. Kal bhī mahfil se terī ham na ṭale, baiṭh gaë, Bole 'uṭh, uṭh' sabhī, yāṅtak ki gale baiṭh gaë . The persistent lover. Even yesterday from your assembly I would not turn out, I stuck all through though all were hoarse with crying out, 'out, out'! 3. Khāë ke beryāṅ jamelisi, kām ke beryāṅ ṭari jālisi . Bhoj. At meal time she all ready sits, at working time away she flits. 4. Ṭālā nahīṅ ṭaltā . The inevitable will not be put off. 5. Āj maiṅ bin horī khele to se, nahīṅ jāūṅgī, Banvārī, jo ho so ho; Sāṅchi jānyo kahat hūṅ to se, lākhan bāt banā mat mo se, Ṭarūṅgī na tere ṭāre, jo ho so ho! Holī. Mauj. To-day, Kanhaīyā, I'll not budge, with you I'll Holī play, Come t…

ety: S. तालुकः
def: s.m. The palate, the ridge or slit in the roof of the mouth; — the membraneous part found in new-born infants at the coronal and sagittal commissures; — a disorder in horses, the lampreys

def: s.m. The sole of the foot; the heel
more: n. m. S. तल bottom. The sole of the foot. 1. Talve bhulas gaë . 2. Zabān kī sī kahūṅ, yā talve kī sī? Prov. Shall I speak as my tongue dictates (truth), or as my heel (from interested motives). talvā khujlānā, yā khujanā , v. n. lit . To itch (the sole of the foot). It is considered a sign of an impending journey. talvoṅ se lagnā , v. n. lit . To touch the sole of the foot. A pimple on an old face see, for small boys such a joke! he—he ! 3. Bachcha baṛe tamāshe kartā hai! What a funny child! 4. Tum bhī baṛe tamāshe ke āḍmī ho . ety: for A. تعمية taʻmiyat, fem., inf. n. ii of عمي 'to be blind'
def: s.m. Rendering blind; making the meaning (of a verse, or of speech, &c.) obscure, covert, or enigmatical; an enigma.
more: (inf. ii. of عمي) s. m. 1. Act of rendering blind. 2. Concealing the meaning of a sentence or discourse. (Generally applied to a chronogram after the manner of abjad, when the clause, which should give the date, fails in accuracy, and the excess or defect is obscurely indicated by another word. Ex. gr. ازحوض لطيف اب بردار az ḥauẓ-i-lat̤īf āb bardār, Draw water from the pellucid cistern. The letters in حوض لطيف make 943, from which substracting those of اب 3, we have the date required 940). 3. An enigma.

ety: for A. تمييز tamyīz, inf. n. ii of ميز 'to separate,' &c.

Observance of the rules of etiquette. tamīz-dār , adj. 1. Discerning; judicious; discreet. 2. Well-conducted; mannerly; well-mannered. tamīz k . v. a. To distinguish; perceive; discern; appreciate. be-tamīz, bad-tamīz , adj. 1. Undiscerning; injudicious; indiscreet; indiscriminate; without distinction. 2. Rude; unmannerly.
(for تمييز inf. ii. of ميز) s. f. Discernment, judgment, discretion, distinction, sense. حرف تميز ḥarf-i-tamīz, An adverb.
subs: adj. Possessing discernment, discerning, judicious, discreet, sensible; well-conducted, well-mannered
(-kī?), To distinguish, to discriminate (between); to discern, perceive
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian تمیز (tamīz), itself from Arabic تَمْيِيز (tamyīz).

def: s.m. A rough kind of sedan chair carried by two men, a 'tonjon' (see thām-jān).
more: n. m. A Native conveyance with two poles attached to both ends and carried over two men's shoulders. 1. Lagā tām-jhām! 2. Baṛhā tām-jhām! Players. 1. Bring the tonjon. 2. Go on. It is related of a man who became the fortunate owner of a tonjon that he used to ride about in it upon the slightest occasion. Thus, having determined to do his own marketing to defeat the rogueries of his servant, the tonjon was continually pressed into service. 'I want chillies,' says the wife. 'Bring the tonjon,' says her husband. 'Oh! I forgot the salt.' 'Bring the tonjon.'
